series_statement.series_info: Kruger, L. Tobacco and Waterpipe Use Survey 2013 [dataset]. Version 1. Cape Town: Economics of Tobacco Control Project [producer], 2018. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2018. DOI: https://doi.org/1025858/8hcg-1h77

version_statement.version: v1: Edited anonymised data for public access

study_info.abstract: The aim of this study was to determine the prevalence of the use of tobacco products and waterpipes and to investigate the associated correlates, in particular perceptions and beliefs about the adverse health consequences of waterpipe use and its social acceptability.  The study involved the administration of an email survey to all students at the four resident universities in the Western Cape Province of South Africa during September 2013.  4578 students completed the survey (4.3% response rate). The Western Cape Universities are Stellenbosch University (SU), Cape Peninsula University of Technology (CPUT), the University of Cape Town (UCT), and the University of the Western Cape (UWC).
study_info.geog_coverage: The survey was specific to four universities in the Western Cape Province of South Africa
study_info.universe: The universe of interest was students at four Western Cape universities.
study_info.notes: The survey collected data on students' age, sex, race, religion, language, ownership of certain items, fee sources, and physical activity. Data was also collected on attitudes to and use of alchohol, marijuana, tobacco products and waterpipes.

dataset_use.cit_req: Kruger, L. Tobacco and Waterpipe Use Survey 2013 [dataset]. Version 1. Cape Town: Economics of Tobacco Control Project [producer], 2018. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2018. DOI: https://doi.org/1025858/8hcg-1h77
dataset_use.conditions: Public use files, available to all
